title: Today's AI Brief: Google's Gemini Spark and Data Center Drama
summary: Google's 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark is put to the test, and environmental activist Erin Brockovich takes on data center secrecy.
imageAlt: Image from source article for I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
imageCaption: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
imageSourceTitle: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful

body.text: I've been digging into Google's new 24/7 AI assistant, Gemini Spark, and I have to say, it's pretty useful. Designed to help navigate your digital life, it summarizes long reads and gets your online tasks done. I put it to work and was impressed with the results. For instance, it's great at condensing lengthy articles into bite-sized chunks, which is perfect for someone like me who's always on the go.

According to TechCrunch, Gemini Spark is Google's latest attempt to make AI-powered assistance more accessible. What stands out to me is its ability to work around the clock, helping with tasks that usually pile up. It's like having a diligent personal assistant who doesn't need coffee breaks. I can see this being a game-changer for people who struggle to keep up with their digital to-do lists.

On a different note, environmental activist Erin Brockovich is taking aim at data center secrecy. With concerns about the environmental impact of these centers growing, her efforts highlight the need for more transparency in the tech industry. It's a story that's been flying under the radar, but it's about to get a lot more attention. The tech industry has a lot to answer for when it comes to its environmental footprint, and I'm curious to see how this story unfolds.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
